panels.description: Wide angle shot: Jim Hawkins peering through a crack into the blockhouse interior, illuminated by a harsh, flickering red torchlight. The interior is cluttered with crates, a large cognac cask, and supply stores. No prisoners are visible. High contrast, ominous shadows.

bubbles.text: The worst of my apprehensions realized... the stores are theirs, but where are the others?

panels.description: Medium shot of the six buccaneers in the dim cabin. Five are standing, flushed and disheveled from sleep/drunkenness. The sixth sits on the floor, propped on an elbow, face deadly pale with a blood-stained bandage wrapped around his head.

Medium close-up of Long John Silver sitting on a crate. His expression is stern and pale. He wears a fine, tattered, mud-stained broadcloth suit. Captain Flint the parrot is perched on his shoulder, preening her feathers.

Split composition: Long John Silver sitting comfortably on a brandy cask, lighting a pipe with composure. In the foreground, Jim Hawkins is shoved against the wall, staring back at Silver with a stoic, brave expression hiding intense internal fear.

bubbles.text: I stood there, looking Silver in the face, with black despair in my heart.
